skill.name: deployment-management
skill.description: Track and manage UniFi deployments across your infrastructure. Monitor deployment status, configuration, and progress for coordinated infrastructure updates.
skill.summary: Monitor UniFi deployment status, configuration, progress, and health across infrastructure sites.
skill.author: DataKnifeAI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown tool names as shell execution and deployment status wording as reconnaissance. Review found no executable code, no shell invocation, no cryptographic implementation, and no prompt injection attempt in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Tool Names Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The flagged lines describe MCP-style tool names and workflow steps in markdown. They do not execute shell commands, interpolate user input, or define scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is plain markdown documentation with backticked tool identifiers. No executable Ruby, shell syntax, or command construction appears at these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Finding
low_findings.description: The flagged description line explains deployment tracking and configuration monitoring. It does not mention or implement any cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is frontmatter text about UniFi deployment management. No cryptographic function, hash name, key handling, or encryption behavior is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Wording in Deployment Context
low_findings.description: The flagged lines use monitoring and progress terms for deployment operations. They do not instruct port scanning, host discovery, network probing, or unauthorized enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding content is about deployment status reporting. No network scanning target, probing method, or external discovery behavior is described.

content.value_statement: Infrastructure teams need clear visibility into active UniFi deployments and configuration changes. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code summarize deployment status, progress, health, and planning context.
actual_capabilities: Lists active deployments across UniFi sites using the documented deployment tools.
Retrieves detailed status and configuration information for a selected deployment.
Summarizes deployment progress, health, and relevant metrics for operators.
Supports planning by showing current deployment load and active change activity.
Helps create deployment status reports and change history summaries.
limitations: The skill documentation does not define rollback execution or automated remediation steps.
It depends on available UniFi deployment data and configured tool access.
It does not include custom scripts, dashboards, or standalone monitoring services.
It provides operational guidance but does not replace production change approval.

use_cases.title: Monitor active rollout health
use_cases.description: Review current UniFi deployments, progress, status, and health before escalating issues.

use_cases.title: Plan maintenance windows
use_cases.description: Check existing deployment load and coordinate new infrastructure updates around active work.

use_cases.title: Document configuration changes
use_cases.description: Collect deployment details and summarize configuration changes for audit and change records.

prompt_templates.title: List current deployments
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me all active UniFi deployments and summarize their current status.

prompt_templates.title: Review one deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: Get details for the selected deployment and explain its progress, configuration, and health.

prompt_templates.title: Find problem deployments
prompt_templates.prompt: List deployments that appear failed, stalled, or unhealthy, then explain the likely operational impact.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a change report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a concise deployment report covering active changes, affected sites, progress, risks, and recommended next checks.

output_examples.output: A deployment overview with active items, current status, progress indicators, and a short health summary for each deployment.
A prioritized list of deployments needing attention, with status context, likely impact, and suggested follow-up checks.
A readable summary of recent deployment activity, configuration changes, deployment outcomes, and items requiring documentation.

best_practices: Check deployment status before starting new infrastructure updates.
Document configuration changes and deployment outcomes after each rollout.
Use maintenance windows and rollback planning for production deployments.
anti_patterns: Do not treat status summaries as approval to bypass change control.
Do not start overlapping deployments without checking current deployment load.
Do not ignore failed or stalled deployments before planning new updates.

faq.question: What systems does this skill focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on UniFi deployment tracking, status review, configuration details, and deployment reporting.

faq.question: Can it list all active deployments?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents a list_deployments tool for viewing deployments across sites.

faq.question: Can it inspect a specific deployment?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents a get_deployment_details tool for detailed deployment status and configuration information.

faq.question: Does it perform deployment rollbacks?
faq.answer: No. The documentation recommends rollback planning but does not define an automated rollback capability.

faq.question: Is this useful for change management?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help summarize deployment history, configuration changes, status, and operational impact.

faq.question: Which AI tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
